title: ribes uva-crispa subsp. grossularia
intro: Ribes uva-crispa subsp. grossularia, also known as the black currant or cassis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Grossulariaceae. It is native to Europe and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ribes uva-crispa subsp. grossularia include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, improve cognitive function, and provide antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and jams, and it is also used as a flavoring agent in foods and beverages.
